title: Cloudflare Configuration Standards
intent: Canonical Cloudflare configuration standards for this runtime. These standards tell agents how to shape changes, not just how to execute them.

universal.title: Use cfctl as the public control plane
universal.summary: Agents should use cfctl for reads, writes, verification, and evidence. Direct backend scripts are maintainer-only.
universal.why: This preserves one trust path, one evidence path, and one mutation policy surface.

universal.title: Use the dev lane first
universal.summary: Default to CF_DEV_TOKEN. Switch to CF_TOKEN_LANE=global only when the dev lane is blocked or the operation intentionally needs the wider emergency surface.
universal.why: This keeps normal work on the narrower lane and makes emergency-scope work explicit.

universal.title: Preview before mutation
universal.summary: Real writes must run with --plan first, then re-run with --ack-plan <operation-id>.
universal.why: This binds the applied change to a reviewed plan and keeps a durable audit artifact.

universal.title: Verify after meaningful change
universal.summary: After a write, perform a targeted readback or use the runtime's verification path.
universal.why: Inventory alone is not proof that the intended resource state actually changed.

universal.title: Keep secret material out of the repo
universal.summary: Secrets stay in ~/dev/.env or approved secret sinks. Runtime artifacts and state specs must remain redacted.
universal.why: This repo is an operational bank, not a credential store.

universal.title: Promote repeatable resources to desired state
universal.summary: When drift is common or the resource is foundational, prefer diff/sync over repeated ad hoc mutation.
universal.why: Desired state is the cleanest path for recurring DNS, Access, and tunnel topology.

standards.title: Declare the application identity shape explicitly
standards.summary: Application domain, type, and self-hosted posture should be explicit rather than inherited by assumption.

standards.title: Keep allowed identity providers deliberate
standards.summary: Self-hosted applications should not drift into implicit or duplicate IdP allowance. allowed_idps should reflect intentional policy.

standards.title: Manage durable Access apps in state/access.app
standards.summary: Foundational apps should be diffed and reconciled through desired state instead of hand-edited repeatedly.

access.policy.stance: desired-state preferred for shared and high-value policies

standards.title: Prefer reusable policies for shared intent
standards.summary: When policy intent is reused across multiple apps, make it reusable instead of cloning near-identical policies.

standards.title: Keep include/exclude/require logic explicit
standards.summary: Policy match structure should be intentional and readable. Do not rely on remembered dashboard defaults.

standards.title: Store durable policies in state/access.policy
standards.summary: Stable policies should be diffable and syncable from desired state.

dns.record.stance: desired-state preferred for repeated, platform, and routing-critical records

standards.title: Set TTL deliberately
standards.summary: Do not rely on implicit TTL assumptions. Critical records should use an explicit TTL that matches the intended failover posture.

standards.title: Set proxy posture explicitly
standards.summary: A/AAAA/CNAME records should declare whether they are proxied. Do not assume the current proxy posture is safe.

standards.title: Classify with full selectors
standards.summary: Permission and safety checks for DNS require zone, name, and type. Partial selectors are not enough to claim lane truth.

standards.title: Move durable routing records into state/dns.record
standards.summary: Records that define app routing, verification, or infrastructure topology should be diffed and synced instead of repeatedly hand-edited.

logpush.job.stance: explicit destination validation and scope control

standards.title: Validate every destination
standards.summary: Logpush jobs should validate ownership and destination reachability before live enablement.

standards.title: Keep account or zone scope deliberate
standards.summary: Logpush jobs should make their scope explicit and avoid overlapping, undocumented collectors.

standards.title: Treat the app repo as the Pages source of truth
standards.summary: This runtime manages Cloudflare posture and inventory, but Pages code and build configuration stay in the application repo.

standards.title: Keep pages_build_output_dir explicit
standards.summary: Pages projects should declare their output directory in config so deployment posture is legible without dashboard memory.

standards.title: Document Functions ownership and secret expectations
standards.summary: If a Pages project uses Functions, the repo should make the ownership and secret path legible to arriving agents.

standards.title: Document attached domains and environment intent
standards.summary: Project domains, preview posture, and environment linkage should be visible in inventory or source-repo docs.

tunnel.stance: desired-state preferred for durable tunnel topology

standards.title: Prefer remote-managed tunnels
standards.summary: Treat Cloudflare-managed configuration as the default. Local tunnel process state is runtime, not the source of truth.

standards.title: Keep config source explicit
standards.summary: Tunnel configuration source, routes, and intended ownership should be explicit and inventoried.

standards.title: Represent durable tunnels under state/tunnel
standards.summary: Long-lived tunnel topology belongs in diff/sync workflows.

standards.title: Document the widget owner and purpose
standards.summary: Each widget should map to a known domain, flow, or product purpose.

standards.title: Keep secret rotation operationally ready
standards.summary: Widgets should have a known rotation path and a verification step for downstream consumers.

standards.title: Treat waiting-room changes as high-risk
standards.summary: Waiting room changes should be previewed carefully because they can alter public traffic flow immediately.

standards.title: Prefer clearly reversible edits
standards.summary: Operational changes should preserve a clean rollback path and avoid multi-variable surprise edits.

standards.title: Treat the source repo as the Worker code source of truth
standards.summary: This runtime inventories and governs Cloudflare posture, but Worker code and wrangler config should live with the Worker source.

standards.title: Bindings should be explicit and named intentionally
standards.summary: D1/KV/R2/Queue/AI bindings should be deliberate and discoverable, not inferred from undocumented deploy state.

worker.runtime.stance: explicit runtime posture and public exposure

standards.title: Keep compatibility_date explicit
standards.summary: Every active Worker or Pages config should pin a compatibility_date so runtime changes are deliberate.

standards.title: Use compatibility flags deliberately
standards.summary: compatibility_flags should appear only when the runtime mode is intentional and understood.

standards.title: Keep workers_dev and public exposure explicit
standards.summary: If a Worker is reachable on workers.dev or through custom domains, that exposure should be intentional and visible in config.

standards.title: Make source-map posture deliberate
standards.summary: Operational Workers should decide explicitly whether upload_source_maps is enabled.

worker.build.stance: reproducible build chain and explicit static assets

standards.title: Keep the Worker build command explicit
standards.summary: Worker builds should declare the build path in config or the repo's deploy wrapper so arriving agents do not guess.

standards.title: Avoid floating installs inside build commands
standards.summary: Build commands should prefer checked-in or pinned toolchain steps instead of network-dependent floating installs.

standards.title: Keep asset directory and handling explicit
standards.summary: If a Worker serves static assets, the directory and any binding or HTML handling should be explicit.

worker.d1.stance: explicit structural binding with deliberate preview posture

standards.title: Keep D1 binding identity explicit
standards.summary: Each D1 binding should declare its binding name, database_name, and database_id.

standards.title: Make preview database posture deliberate
standards.summary: Preview-aware app Workers should decide explicitly whether preview_database_id matches production or uses a dedicated preview database.

standards.title: Keep migrations ownership explicit
standards.summary: If D1 schema migration is part of the repo contract, migrations_dir or the equivalent migration path should be legible.

worker.errors.stance: fail-closed behavior with observable, debuggable failure paths

standards.title: Fail closed on auth, routing, and policy errors
standards.summary: Public Cloudflare workers should return a deliberate failure path when auth, config, or required assets are unavailable. They should not silently degrade into a misleading success path.

standards.title: Keep 4xx and 5xx behavior deliberate
standards.summary: User-facing Workers should distinguish caller error from server or upstream failure, and should not leak raw stack traces or secrets in responses.

standards.title: Active workers should have explicit observability
standards.summary: If a Worker serves traffic, runs on cron, or fronts a container, logs and failure diagnostics should be explicit in config.

standards.title: Make debuggability deliberate
standards.summary: JavaScript and TypeScript Workers should decide explicitly whether upload_source_maps is enabled so real failures can be debugged without guesswork.

standards.title: Treat scheduled error paths as idempotent work
standards.summary: Cron-triggered or retryable background work should be safe to rerun and should log enough context to distinguish retry noise from real drift.

worker.routes.stance: explicit domain ownership and deliberate exposure mode

standards.title: Keep route and domain ownership explicit
standards.summary: Custom domains, route patterns, and workers_dev exposure should be legible in config without dashboard memory.

standards.title: Review dual exposure deliberately
standards.summary: If a Worker is reachable on workers.dev and a routed domain, that dual exposure should be intentional.

worker.vars.stance: plain deploy vars only, never secrets

standards.title: Keep secrets out of [vars]
standards.summary: Secret-bearing values belong in secrets or external sinks, not in plain-text wrangler vars.

standards.title: Use placeholders only with a real render owner
standards.summary: If [vars] contain placeholders, the repo should have an explicit render or deploy contract that owns them.

standards.title: Document the plain-text deploy-var contract
standards.summary: Non-secret vars should still have clear ownership and intent.

worker.observability.stance: public and scheduled workers should be observable by default

standards.title: Prefer explicit observability on public, cron, and container workers
standards.summary: If a Worker serves public traffic, runs on cron, or fronts a container, logs and traces posture should be explicit.

standards.title: Keep logs and traces deliberate
standards.summary: Sampling, persistence, and invocation log posture should be intentional rather than left to remembered defaults.

standards.title: Keep cron schedules explicit
standards.summary: Scheduled workers should declare their cron shape clearly in config or a documented render path.

standards.title: Scheduled work should be idempotent by contract
standards.summary: A cron-triggered Worker should document or encode why reruns are safe.

standards.title: Placeholder cron values need a named owner
standards.summary: If a cron value is a deploy-time placeholder, the render path should be explicit.

worker.containers.stance: digest-pinned deploy images or explicit repo-local Dockerfile ownership

standards.title: Make the container image source explicit
standards.summary: Container-backed Workers should use digest-pinned registry images or an explicit repo-local Dockerfile build definition. Mutable tag refs should not be the deploy contract.

standards.title: Keep Durable Object bindings explicit
standards.summary: Container-backed DO classes, binding names, and migrations should be legible in config.

standards.title: Keep rollout posture explicit
standards.summary: rollout_step_percentage and grace posture should be intentional for container Workers.

worker.services.stance: explicit service binding names and downstream ownership

standards.title: Keep service binding names and targets explicit
standards.summary: Worker service bindings should declare both their binding name and target service clearly.

standards.title: Make downstream ownership legible
standards.summary: When a router Worker binds downstream services, the repo should make those services and ownership boundaries legible to arriving agents.

worker.storage.stance: explicit binding names and deliberate raw-credential exceptions

standards.title: Keep storage binding names and targets explicit
standards.summary: R2 bindings should declare their binding and bucket_name clearly.

standards.title: Treat raw credential access as an explicit exception
standards.summary: If a repo bypasses Worker bindings and uses raw storage credentials, that should be documented as an intentional exception.
